# Firebase Storage Security Rules

Copy and paste the rules below into your [Firebase Console](https://console.firebase.google.com/) under **Storage > Rules**.

```javascript
rules_version = '2';
service firebase.storage {
  match /b/{bucket}/o {
    
    // User Avatars
    // Only the user can upload their own avatar, but anyone can view them.
    match /users/{userId}/avatar/{fileName} {
      allow read: if true;
      allow write: if request.auth != null && request.auth.uid == userId;
    }

    // User Assets (Images, Videos, Audio generated by nodes)
    match /users/{userId}/assets/{fileName} {
      // Anyone can read (so public workflows load assets correctly)
      allow read: if true;
      
      // Only the authenticated owner can upload, update, or delete
      allow write: if request.auth != null && request.auth.uid == userId;
    }

    // Workflow Thumbnails
    match /users/{userId}/workflows/{workflowId}/thumbnail/{fileName} {
      allow read: if true;
      allow write: if request.auth != null && request.auth.uid == userId;
    }
  }
}
```

## Storage Structure Overview
Based on the `src/services/storageService.ts`, the app stores files in the following structure:
1. `users/{userId}/avatar/` - User profile pictures.
2. `users/{userId}/assets/` - Uploaded images and media generated by nodes.
3. `users/{userId}/workflows/{workflowId}/thumbnail/` - Preview thumbnails for saved workflows.

## Troubleshooting
- If thumbnails or assets are failing to upload, ensure the user is logged in.
- If assets aren't loading when viewing a public workflow, double-check that the `read` permission on `users/{userId}/assets/{fileName}` is set to `true`.
